allOutcomes.title: CV Death or Worsening Heart Failure
Renal Composite (≥50% eGFR Decline, ESRD, or Renal Death)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: McMurray JJV et al. NEJM 2019;381:1995-2008, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 4,744 patients underwent randomization, with 2,373 assigned to dapagliflozin and 2,371 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome
evidence.source: McMurray JJV et al. NEJM 2019;381:1995-2008, Table 2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event (worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death) occurred in 386 of 2,373 patients (16.3%) in the dapagliflozin group and in 502 of 2,371 patients (21.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.65 to 0.85).

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter phase III trial with adjudicated cardiovascular endpoints.

evidence.label: Secondary Outcomes
evidence.source: McMurray JJV et al. NEJM 2019;381:1995-2008, Table 2
evidence.text: CV death occurred in 227 (9.6%) vs 273 (11.5%) patients (HR 0.82; 95% CI 0.69-0.98). All-cause mortality: 276 (11.6%) vs 329 (13.9%) (HR 0.83; 0.71-0.97). First HF hospitalization: 237 (10.0%) vs 326 (13.7%) (HR 0.70; 0.59-0.83).

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline
evidence.source: McMurray JJV et al. NEJM 2019;381:1995-2008, Table 1
evidence.text: Median age 66 years; 76.2% male; mean LVEF 31.2%; 41.8% had T2DM; median NT-proBNP 1,437 pg/mL. 94% on ACEi/ARB/ARNI, 96% beta-blocker, 71% MRA.

evidence.label: Pharmacology & Mechanism
evidence.text: Selective SGLT2 inhibitor (IC50 1.12 nM at SGLT2). Promotes glucosuria and natriuresis, reducing preload and cardiac filling pressures. Initial eGFR dip (3–5 mL/min) due to tubuloglomerular feedback reverses within weeks; net long-term renoprotection. Cardioprotective mechanisms include improved myocardial energetics, reduced inflammation, and enhanced autophagy.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance
evidence.text: Primary composite (CV death or worsening HF) events verified against ClinicalTrials.gov structured results. Treatment arm: 386/2,373; Control arm: 502/2,371. Event counts match published Table 2.

evidence.label: CT.gov API v2 Validation
evidence.text: HR values cross-validated against CT.gov structured results (Cox regression, stratified by T2DM). Primary: HR 0.74 (0.65–0.85, p<0.0001). CV Death: HR 0.82 (implied). ACM: HR 0.83 (0.71–0.97, p=0.0217). Renal composite: HR 0.71 (0.44–1.16). All concordant with NEJM publication.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 130 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Provision of signed informed consent prior to any study specific procedures~* Male or female, aged ≥18 years~* Established documented diagnosis of symptomatic HFrEF (NYHA functional class II-IV), which has been present for at least 2 months~* LVEF≤40%~* Elevated NT-proBNP levels~* Patients should receive background standard of care for HFrEF and be treated according to locally recognized guidelines~* eGFR ≥30 mL/min/1.73 m^2 (CKD-EPI formula) at enrolment (visit 1)~Exclus...

allOutcomes.title: CV Death or Heart Failure Hospitalization
Renal Composite (≥50% eGFR Decline, ESRD, or Renal Death)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Packer M et al. NEJM 2020;383:1413-1424, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 3,730 patients were randomized, with 1,863 assigned to empagliflozin and 1,867 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome
evidence.source: Packer M et al. NEJM 2020;383:1413-1424, Table 2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event (cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure) occurred in 361 of 1,863 patients (19.4%) in the empagliflozin group and in 462 of 1,867 patients (24.7%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.75; 95% CI, 0.65 to 0.86).

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter phase III trial with central endpoint adjudication.

evidence.label: Secondary Outcomes
evidence.source: Packer M et al. NEJM 2020;383:1413-1424, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline
evidence.source: Packer M et al. NEJM 2020;383:1413-1424, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 67.2 years; 76.1% male; mean LVEF 27.5%; 49.8% had T2DM; median NT-proBNP 1,907 pg/mL. 70% on ARNI/ACEi/ARB, 95% beta-blocker, 71% MRA.

evidence.label: Pharmacology & Mechanism
evidence.text: Selective SGLT2 inhibitor. IC50 1.3 nM at SGLT2 vs 8,300 nM at SGLT1 (6,400-fold selectivity). Reduces intravascular volume via osmotic diuresis without reflex neurohormonal activation.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance
evidence.text: Primary composite events verified against structured results. Treatment: 361/1,863; Control: 462/1,867. Concordant with published data.

evidence.label: CT.gov API v2 Validation
evidence.text: HR values cross-validated against CT.gov structured results (Cox regression). Primary: HR 0.75 (0.65–0.86, p<0.0001). First HHF: HR 0.69 (0.59–0.81). CV Death: HR 0.92 (0.75–1.12). ACM: HR 0.92 (0.77–1.10). Renal composite: HR 0.50 (0.32–0.77, p=0.0019). All concordant.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Male or female patient age >= 18 years at screening. For Japan only: Age >= 20 years at screening~* Patients with chronic HF (Chronic Heart Failure) NYHA (New York Heart Association Classification) class II-IV and reduced EF (Ejection Fraction) (LVEF (Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction) <=40%) and elevated NT-proBNP (N-terminal of the prohormone brain natriuretic peptide)~  * If EF >= 36% to <= 40%: NT-proBNP >= 2500 pg/ml or patients without AF (atrial fibrillation/...

allOutcomes.title: CV Death or Heart Failure Hospitalization
Renal Composite (≥50% eGFR Decline, ESRD, or Renal Death)

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2021;385:1451-1461, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 5,988 patients were randomized, with 2,997 assigned to empagliflozin and 2,991 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome
evidence.source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2021;385:1451-1461, Table 2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event (cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure) occurred in 415 of 2,997 patients (13.8%) in the empagliflozin group and in 511 of 2,991 patients (17.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.79; 95% CI, 0.69 to 0.90).

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter phase III trial in HF with LVEF >40% and blinded adjudication of primary events.

evidence.label: Secondary Outcomes
evidence.source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2021;385:1451-1461, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline
evidence.source: Anker SD et al. NEJM 2021;385:1451-1461, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 71.9 years; 55.3% male; median LVEF 54%; 49.0% had T2DM; median NT-proBNP 994 pg/mL. Broader EF spectrum than EMPEROR-Reduced (HFpEF and HFmrEF).

evidence.label: Pharmacology Note
evidence.text: First SGLT2i to demonstrate benefit in HFpEF, extending the class effect across the full EF spectrum. Dose: 10 mg once daily, same as HFrEF indication.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance
evidence.text: Treatment: 415/2,997; Control: 511/2,991. Primary outcome counts match NEJM publication Table 2.

evidence.label: CT.gov API v2 Validation
evidence.text: HR values cross-validated against CT.gov structured results (Cox regression). Primary: HR 0.79 (0.69–0.90, p=0.0003). First HHF: HR 0.71 (0.60–0.83). CV Death: HR 0.91 (0.76–1.09). ACM: HR 1.00 (0.87–1.15). Renal: HR 0.95 (0.73–1.24). All concordant.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Male or female patient, age >= 18 years at screening. For Japan only: Age >=20 years at screening~* Patients with chronic HF (Chronic Heart Failure) NYHA (New York Heart Association classification) class II-IV and preserved EF (Ejection Fraction)(LVEF (Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction) > 40 %) and elevated NT-proBNP (N-terminal of the prohormone brain natriuretic peptide) > 300 pg/ml for patients without AF, OR > 900 pg/ml for patients with AF, analysed at the Centr...

allOutcomes.title: CV Death or Worsening Heart Failure

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2022;387:1089-1098, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 6,263 patients were randomized, with 3,131 assigned to dapagliflozin and 3,132 assigned to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome
evidence.source: Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2022;387:1089-1098, Table 2
evidence.text: A primary outcome event (worsening heart failure or cardiovascular death) occurred in 512 of 3,131 patients (16.4%) in the dapagliflozin group and in 610 of 3,132 patients (19.5%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.82; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.92).

evidence.label: RoB Assessment Basis
evidence.text: Randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter phase III trial in HF with mildly reduced or preserved ejection fraction.

evidence.label: Secondary Outcomes
evidence.source: Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2022;387:1089-1098, Table 2

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline
evidence.source: Solomon SD et al. NEJM 2022;387:1089-1098, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 71.7 years; 55.5% male; median LVEF 54%; 44.7% had T2DM; median NT-proBNP 1,126 pg/mL. Included both de novo and previously hospitalized HF patients.

evidence.label: Pharmacology Note
evidence.text: Second dapagliflozin HF trial (after DAPA-HF). Confirmed benefit in HFmrEF/HFpEF. DELIVER and DAPA-HF together provide evidence across the full LVEF spectrum for dapagliflozin.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance
evidence.text: Treatment: 512/3,131; Control: 610/3,132. Verified against published results and ClinicalTrials.gov structured data.

evidence.label: CT.gov API v2 Validation
evidence.text: HR values cross-validated against CT.gov structured results (Cox regression, stratified by T2DM). Primary: HR 0.82 (0.73–0.92, p=0.0008). CV Death: HR 0.88 (0.74–1.05). ACM: HR 0.94 (0.83–1.07). Recurrent CV death + HF: Rate Ratio 0.77 (0.67–0.89). All concordant.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 40 Years; max age 130 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~1. Provision of signed informed consent prior to any study specific procedures.~2. Male or female patients age ≥40 years.~3. Documented diagnosis of symptomatic heart failure (NYHA class II-IV) at enrolment, and a medical history of typical symptoms/signs of heart failure ≥6 weeks before enrolment with at least intermittent need for diuretic treatment.~4. Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction (LVEF) >40% and evidence of structural heart disease (i.e. left ventricular hypertrop...

NCT03521934.name: SOLOIST-WHF

allOutcomes.title: CV Death or Total HF Events (Hospitalization/Urgent Visits) — recurrent-event estimand
HF Hospitalizations/Urgent Visits (Total Events; recurrent estimand)

NCT03521934.snippet: Source: N Engl J Med 2021;384:117-128. RoB: D3 some (early termination, 30% enrollment); D5 some (primary endpoint changed from first-event to recurrent-event during trial).

evidence.label: Enrollment & Randomization
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. N Engl J Med 2021;384:117-128, CONSORT
evidence.text: A total of 1,222 patients were randomized, with 608 assigned to sotagliflozin and 614 to placebo.

evidence.label: Primary Composite Outcome (Recurrent Framework)
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. N Engl J Med 2021;384:117-128, Table 2
evidence.text: Primary endpoint was total number of cardiovascular deaths, hospitalizations for heart failure, and urgent visits for heart failure: 245 events in the sotagliflozin group vs 355 in placebo (hazard ratio/rate-ratio framework 0.67; 95% CI, 0.52 to 0.85).

evidence.label: Estimand Compatibility Note
evidence.text: SOLOIST-WHF primary endpoint is recurrent-event based. Use in HR/RR sensitivity mode; do not pool as 2x2 first-event OR/RR.

evidence.label: Secondary Outcomes
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. N Engl J Med 2021;384:117-128, Table 2
evidence.text: CV death: 51 (8.4%) vs 58 (9.4%). All-cause mortality: 65 (10.7%) vs 76 (12.4%). Trial stopped early for loss of funding (COVID-19 pandemic) after median 9.2 months follow-up.

evidence.label: Demographics & Baseline
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. N Engl J Med 2021;384:117-128, Table 1
evidence.text: Mean age 69.0 years; 66.4% male; mean LVEF 35%; 100% had T2DM (by design); median NT-proBNP 1,803 pg/mL. All patients had recent worsening HF (hospitalization or urgent visit within 2 days before or during index admission).

evidence.label: Pharmacology & Mechanism
evidence.source: Bhatt DL et al. N Engl J Med 2020;382:1419-1430 (SCORED)
evidence.text: Sotagliflozin is a dual SGLT1/SGLT2 inhibitor (IC50 36 nM SGLT1 vs 1.8 nM SGLT2). Unlike selective SGLT2i, it also inhibits intestinal glucose absorption (SGLT1), potentially offering additional glycemic and GI benefits.

evidence.label: Endpoint Concordance
evidence.text: Treatment: 245 total events/608 patients; Control: 355 total events/614 patients. Note: these are total events (recurrent framework), not first-event counts. First-event binary 2x2 not directly extractable.

evidence.label: CT.gov API v2 Validation
evidence.text: HR values cross-validated against CT.gov structured results (Cox proportional hazards). Primary: HR 0.67 (0.52–0.85, p<0.001). HHF+Urgent: HR 0.64 (0.49–0.83). CV Death: HR 0.84 (0.58–1.22). ACM: HR 0.82 (0.59–1.14). All concordant. Trial terminated early due to funding loss (COVID-19).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 85 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion criteria:~* Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus.~* Admitted to the hospital, or urgent heart failure visit for worsening heart failure.~* Prior diagnosis of heart failure (> 3 months).~* Prior chronic treatment for heart failure with a loop diuretic (eg furosemide, torsemide, bumetanide) for > 30 days.~* Randomized when hemodynamically stable, prior to hospital discharge or within 3 days of discharge.~* Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) ≥150 picograms per milliliter (pg/mL) (≥450 pg/mL for parti...
